ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for raw minced/ground chicken; closest defensible match is 'chicken meat food product' (FOODON_03311349). Minced/ground form is not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for raw minced chicken as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for granular food preparation forms is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw minced chicken is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a small palm-sized portion used in stir-fries, patties, or stuffed dishes before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Minced chicken, raw — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for minced chicken, raw (cr_id F11010039). All core macros and micronutrients are exact matches from this primary regional source. saturated_fat_g of 0.3 g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central (NDB 05298, raw ground chicken) as the HPB entry does not separately report saturated fat fractions.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate protein food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw minced chicken contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned. gi_category is set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirms no GI test exists for this food type, consistent with the not_applicable classification for pure protein foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (49 mg/100 g) and high potassium (360 mg/100 g) supporting a favourable Na:K ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Raw minced chicken is suitable for individuals with hypertension; its very low sodium content and high potassium level support blood pressure management, but sodium should remain controlled during cooking by avoiding high-sodium sauces such as soy sauce or oyster sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content; high protein (21.4 g/100 g) slows gastric emptying and does not raise postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw minced chicken is highly suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content; preparation methods should avoid high-sugar marinades or coatings such as honey, sweet chilli, or teriyaki sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (1.1 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (approximately 0.3 g/100 g); cholesterol is moderate at 82 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw minced chicken is a lean protein source with very low saturated fat, making it appropriate for cardiovascular disease management; cholesterol intake should be monitored in the context of overall dietary pattern, and steaming or poaching is preferred over frying to preserve the lean lipid profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of positive anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3, no antioxidants, no polyphenols, no dietary fibre); lean protein alone is insufficient for RA suitability
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw minced chicken does not meet the positive anti-inflammatory criterion required for rheumatoid arthritis suitability; it should be paired with anti-inflammatory ingredients such as ginger, turmeric, or cruciferous vegetables to create an RA-appropriate meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Minced chicken, raw, is finely ground raw chicken meat displaying a pale pink colour with a soft, malleable texture. It is produced by mechanically or manually grinding chicken cuts — typically breast, thigh, or a combination — into a fine mince without added ingredients. As a raw ingredient, it requires thorough cooking before consumption. Nutritionally, raw minced chicken is a high-protein, very low-fat food providing 97 kcal, 21.4 g protein, and only 1.1 g fat per 100 g, with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar. Its sodium content is low at 49 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 360 mg/100 g, making it well-aligned with DASH dietary principles for blood pressure management. The cholesterol content is moderate at 82 mg/100 g. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes (zero glycaemic impact), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium), and cardiovascular disease (very low saturated fat). It does not provide omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, polyphenols, or dietary fibre, and therefore does not independently meet anti-inflammatory criteria for rheumatoid arthritis management. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Common culinary uses include stir-fries, dumplings, meatballs, stuffed vegetables, patties, and noodle dishes across Asian and Western cuisines.

notes: carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: raw chicken meat contains no carbohydrates — naturally zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for raw chicken. sugar_g is naturally zero as chicken meat contains no sugars. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a pure animal protein product with no plant-derived fibre. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this item; these values are not available in the primary source as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients and water content are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for minced chicken raw (F11010039); detailed vitamin and mineral panel not available in this primary source record. saturated_fat_g of 0.3 g is an estimate consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for raw ground chicken (USDA NDB 05298), as the HPB entry does not separately report saturated fat; this is documented as an adapted value. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_index field is set to numeric 0 per template requirement for numeric hygiene compliance; the not_applicable gi_category and gi_source notes make clear that no GI value is assigned and the numeric 0 is a placeholder sentinel consistent with the zero glycaemic load. This is a generic raw ingredient record; no cuisine-specific multilingual naming is required as minced chicken is not a named dish from a specific cuisine tradition.
